pattern: Starting from index k and widening toward whichever side has the larger neighbor keeps the window minimum as high as possible while it grows.
hints: The window must contain index k, so begin with just that element and expand outward.
At each step extend toward the side whose next value is larger, because that keeps the running minimum higher.
Maintain the current minimum over [left, right]; expand left or right one step toward the bigger boundary value, update the minimum, and track minimum*(right-left+1).

edge_inputs.description: optimal window extends both directions from k
k at the start, best window grows rightward
k value is a local maximum, do not always widen
all equal values, widening always helps
two elements with k on the smaller side

stress.description: random array with a random good index
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 400))
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 20001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, n)
    return [nums, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maximumScore(self, nums, k):
        n = len(nums)
        left = right = k
        mn = nums[k]
        best = mn
        while left > 0 or right < n - 1:
            lv = nums[left - 1] if left > 0 else -1
            rv = nums[right + 1] if right < n - 1 else -1
            if lv >= rv:
                left -= 1
                mn = min(mn, nums[left])
            else:
                right += 1
                mn = min(mn, nums[right])
            best = max(best, mn * (right - left + 1))
        return best
